name: HOUGHALL FARM , DURHAM

surface_od_m.qualifier: absent — 'Ground Level (if known)' box on WR-38 left blank; WR39/2 recovery sheet datum box (p5) carries 'ELV 158FT'

units: metres, transcribed verbatim from typed/handwritten site-investigation record
notes: Water borehole record (BGS Form WR-38, Hydrogeology Research Group) for Houghall Farm Ltd dairy farm, drilled by Dales Water Services Ltd, commenced 21/11/02 completed 26/11/02, drilled to 59 m, agricultural supply. Strata log is on page 2 only (handwritten, thickness/depth in m); all 13 intervals sum cleanly, final depth 59.00 m. No seam names and no pencil seam-correlation marginalia anywhere in the scan. Ground Level box on the WR-38 is blank; the WR39/2 recovery sheet datum box (p5) reads 'ELV 158FT' (~48.2 m), the only surface-elevation hint — datum for water levels is EDGE OF MANHOLE, 60 cm above GL. Water: no 'water struck at' recorded; test pumping (WR-38 sect. C) water level 2.85 mbd at start, 3.35 mbd at end, pumping 16.79 m3/hour for 24 hours, recovery to 3.14 mbd in 12 hrs. Casing: 150 mm plain steel GL to 19.50 m, 125 mm slotted steel 18.50 to 59 m; plain casing annulus pressure grouted. Non-strata pages: p1 WR-38 header/construction/test-pumping form; p3 EA WR39/1(A) constant-rate pumping test readings 4/2/03 (discharge to pond, overflow to River Wear, pump depth 22 m); p4 WR39/4 continuation sheet 5/2/03; p5 WR39/2 recovery readings 5/2/03; p6-7 ALcontrol Laboratories water chemistry Certificate of Analysis (ref 'Houghall College', sampled 05/12/02; iron, manganese, magnesium and total coliforms flagged Fail); p8 handwritten cover note (OUR REF C 6354, E.A. REF CON 24/5/006, 'Borehole drilled for agricultural use on a dairy farm'); p9 photo sheet (test equipment; discharge point to pond, overflow to River Wear). Strata depths assumed below ground level (log does not restate the manhole datum).
